title: NOC 22101 – Geological and Mineral Technologists and Technicians | GTR Immigration
description: Geological and Mineral Technologists and Technicians in Canada play a crucial role in mining, petroleum exploration, environmental protection, and geoscience research. Classified at TEER 2, this occupation is well-aligned with:
duties: Assist metallurgists with corrosion control & welding projects
employment_requirements: 2–3-year college diploma in geological, mining, petroleum, hydrogeology, or metallurgical technology (technologists)
Quebec regulatory-body membership (to use the title “Professional Technologist”)
About two years of supervised work experience
additional_information: Mining & critical-minerals projectsOil & gas explorationEnvironmental remediationInfrastructure developmentNorthern-territory field operations
Annual salaries typically range from $37,200–$105,900 CAD, depending on province and specialization.

Strong demand in resource provincesMultiple employer-sponsored PR routesStable unionized & government rolesOutdoor + technical hybrid careersFamily immigration benefits & healthcareLong-term project pipelines
